content:
# Numbers Chapter 27 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 181-182 | **Source Image:** `NUM_027_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_027_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0134-pa_0135

---

**1** And the daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, of the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph, drew near -- and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah --

**2** and they stood before Moses and before Eleazar the priest and before the leaders and all the congregation, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying,

**3** "Our father died in the wilderness, and he was not among the congregation of those who gathered together against YHWH in the congregation of Korah, but he died in his own sin, and he had no sons.

**4** Why should the name of our father be withdrawn from the midst of his clan because he had no son? Give to us a possession among the brothers of our father."

**5** And Moses brought their case before YHWH.

**6** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**7** "The daughters of Zelophehad speak rightly. You shall surely give to them a possession of an inheritance among the brothers of their father, and you shall cause the inheritance of their father to pass over to them.

**8** And to the sons of Israel you shall speak, saying, 'If a man dies and has no son, then you shall cause his inheritance to pass over to his daughter.

**9** And if he has no daughter, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.

**10** And if he has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to the brothers of his father.

**11** And if his father has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to his kinsman who is nearest to him from his clan, and he shall possess it.' And it shall be for the sons of Israel a statute of judgment, as YHWH commanded Moses."

**12** And YHWH said to Moses, "Go up to this mountain of Abarim and see the land that I have given to the sons of Israel.

**13** And when you have seen it, you also shall be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother was gathered,

**14** because you rebelled against my word in the wilderness of Zin, in the strife of the congregation, to sanctify me at the waters before their eyes." These are the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin.

**15** And Moses spoke to YHWH, saying,

**16** "Let YHWH, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation,

**17** who shall go out before them and who shall come in before them, and who shall lead them out and who shall bring them in, so that the congregation of YHWH may not be as sheep that have no shepherd."

**18** And YHWH said to Moses, "Take for yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is the spirit, and lay your hand upon him.

**19** And set him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation, and commission him before their eyes.

**20** And you shall put some of your splendor upon him, so that all the congregation of the sons of Israel may obey.

**21** And before Eleazar the priest he shall stand, and he shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before YHWH. At his word they shall go out and at his word they shall come in, he and all the sons of Israel with him, and all the congregation."

**22** And Moses did as YHWH commanded him. And he took Joshua and set him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation.

**23** And he laid his hands upon him and commissioned him, as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **Genealogy of Zelophehad (v. 1):** The Hebrew lists a six-generation genealogy ascending from Zelophehad to Joseph. The Leningrad Codex reads בְּנוֹת צְלָפְחָד בֶּן־חֵפֶר בֶּן־גִּלְעָד בֶּן־מָכִיר בֶּן־מְנַשֶּׁה. The phrase "of the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph" (לְמִשְׁפְּחֹת מְנַשֶּׁה בֶן־יוֹסֵף) is rendered literally, preserving the tribal linkage. Vaticanus confirms the genealogy in its Greek rendering.

2. **"In his own sin" (v. 3):** Hebrew בְּחֶטְאוֹ מֵת -- rendered "he died in his own sin," preserving the daughters' distinction that their father was not among the rebels of Korah but died of ordinary mortality/sin. The LXX (Vaticanus) has ἐν τῇ ἁμαρτίᾳ αὐτοῦ ἀπέθανεν, confirming this reading.

3. **"Withdrawn" (v. 4):** Hebrew יִגָּרַע, from גרע, meaning "to diminish, withdraw, be taken away." Rendered "withdrawn" to preserve the passive sense -- the name being diminished from the clan. Some translations use "taken away" but "withdrawn" captures the nuance of gradual erasure from communal memory.

4. **"Speak rightly" (v. 7):** Hebrew כֵּן בְּנוֹת צְלָפְחָד דֹּבְרֹת -- literally "rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak." YHWH's vindication of the daughters' legal claim is rendered with the adverb "rightly" to capture כֵּן in its affirmative juridical sense.

5. **"Statute of judgment" (v. 11):** Hebrew לְחֻקַּת מִשְׁפָּט -- rendered "statute of judgment" preserving both terms. This is legal terminology establishing permanent case law for Israel, not merely a one-time ruling.

6. **"Mountain of Abarim" (v. 12):** Hebrew הַר הָעֲבָרִים -- rendered as a proper name "mountain of Abarim" (the range east of the Jordan). The Vaticanus reads εἰς τὸ ὄρος τὸ ἐν τῷ πέραν, "the mountain in the beyond/crossing," interpreting עֲבָרִים etymologically.

7. **"Spirits of all flesh" (v. 16):** Hebrew אֱלֹהֵי הָרוּחֹת לְכָל־בָּשָׂר -- "the God of the spirits of all flesh." This theologically significant title appears also in Numbers 16:22. It affirms YHWH's sovereignty over the life-breath of every living being, making it fitting as the address in a petition for leadership succession.

8. **"Spirit" (v. 18):** Hebrew רוּחַ -- "a man in whom is the spirit" (אִישׁ אֲשֶׁר־רוּחַ בּוֹ). The absence of the article or possessive in MT leaves this deliberately open -- it may denote the divine spirit, a spirit of wisdom, or simply the quality of inspired leadership. The translation preserves this ambiguity by rendering simply "the spirit."

9. **"Splendor" (v. 20):** Hebrew הוֹדֶךָ -- from הוֹד, meaning "majesty, splendor, glory." Rendered "splendor" rather than "authority" or "honor" to preserve the concrete, visible quality implied. Moses is to invest Joshua with a visible mark of his own God-given dignity. "Some of" translates the partitive מִן -- not all of Moses' splendor is transferred.

10. **"Commission" (vv. 19, 23):** Hebrew צִוָּה -- rendered "commission" throughout this passage, as the context is the formal investiture of Joshua with authority. The same root elsewhere means simply "command," but here the ceremonial and public setting warrants the more specific "commission."

11. **"By the hand of Moses" (v. 23):** Hebrew בְּיַד מֹשֶׁה -- preserved literally rather than paraphrasing as "through Moses," maintaining the Hebrew idiom that links divine speech to human agency.

*-- LOGOS*
